page_title: International Experience Canada: Who can apply - Canada.ca

h3: You can’t participate with a refugee travel document
Get more support with recognized organizations

sections.heading: Who can participate
items: To participate in International Experience Canada (IEC), your country or territory of citizenship must have a youth mobility agreement (YMA) with Canada.

Some countries only allow you to participate once. Others allow you to participate twice, but in different categories. Because each country is different, you should check your country’s eligibility requirements.

sections.heading: You can’t participate with a refugee travel document
items: You’re not eligible to participate in IEC if you hold a refugee travel document from any country .

sections.heading: Get more support with recognized organizations
items: Looking for more support before or during your participation? A recognized organization (RO) might be able to help you work and travel through IEC. Learn more about working and travelling in Canada using an RO .

sections.heading: Canadians ages 18-35
items: Find out how to participate in International Experience Canada to work and travel abroad .
